whatsnew.title: More Reliable Environment Creation
whatsnew.description: Extending or creating an environment (including from imports) now always lands in the right profile and environment node, with the YAML file created on demand — instead of writing to the old flat .env format.

whatsnew.title: Editor & Reliability Fixes
whatsnew.description: The environment editor no longer discards an in-progress variable you were adding when you switch tabs, the file-link "@" picker in multipart tables claims Enter correctly, the code editor fills its panel height properly, and running a documentation-only section now shows a friendly "Nothing to run" message instead of an empty response panel.

whatsnew.title: Block Overview Panel
whatsnew.description: See every block in a file at a glance from a new overview panel, making it easy to jump straight to any section without scrolling through the whole document. Great for long .void files with a lot of sections.

whatsnew.title: Drag-and-Drop Block Reordering
whatsnew.description: Reorder blocks directly from the overview panel by dragging them into place — no more cutting and pasting content to move a section up or down in the file.

whatsnew.title: Unresolved Variable Guard
whatsnew.description: Voiden now blocks a request from sending if it still contains an unresolved {{variable}} after environment and runtime substitution, instead of letting the literal placeholder go out over the wire.

whatsnew.title: Editor Usability Pass
whatsnew.description: Scrollbar behavior, cursor placement, window memory, and select-all all got attention this release, along with smoother keyboard navigation and nesting when moving between blocks.

whatsnew.title: Cookie, Markdown & Tooltip Fixes
whatsnew.description: Fixed set-cookie capture lagging one request behind in runtime variables, markdown formatting leaking inside blocks where it shouldn't render, and the "What's New" tooltip popping up after the mouse had already left the button.

whatsnew.title: Plugin Hot-Reload
whatsnew.description: Install and update community plugins without restarting the app — changes take effect instantly. We rebuilt the plugin loader to swap code in place, so your editor stays open, your tabs stay exactly where you left them, and there's no jarring full-app reload interrupting your flow.

whatsnew.title: Collection Runner
whatsnew.description: Run a sequence of linked .void files in order, straight from the sidebar — perfect for chaining auth, setup, and verification requests into one repeatable API test flow. Each run streams its results live, and you can re-run any step on its own without restarting the whole sequence.

whatsnew.title: Faster Plugin Loading
whatsnew.description: Core extensions now load from pre-bundled files instead of being compiled on the fly, cutting cold-start time significantly — especially noticeable if you run several plugins at once.

whatsnew.title: Editor & Response Fixes
whatsnew.description: Fixed a DOM error that could crash the editor when uninstalling an extension while it was active, and corrected JSON response parsing so large integers keep their full precision instead of being rounded by floating-point conversion.

welcome.headline: Welcome to Voiden
welcome.subheadline: The API client built for developers who live in their editor.

items.title: Void Files
items.description: Write API requests in plain markdown. Version-controlled, shareable, and readable by humans.

items.title: Plugins
items.description: Extend Voiden with community plugins or build your own using the Voiden SDK.

items.title: Environments
items.description: Switch between dev, staging, and production with instant variable interpolation.

items.title: Request History
items.description: Every request is logged. Search, replay, and export to curl in one click.
